name: Jitsu Event Collection API
what_it_does: Open-source event collection and data pipeline platform with REST API — collecting behavioral events from websites and apps and routing them to data warehouses (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift) and destinations, as a self-hosted Segment alternative.
use_cases: Collecting page views, clicks, and custom events via JavaScript SDK or HTTP API
Routing events to multiple destinations (BigQuery, Mixpanel, Amplitude) simultaneously
Self-hosted alternative to Segment with full data ownership and no per-event pricing
Building product analytics pipeline with event collection feeding data warehouse
Replacing Segment with open-source stack for privacy-conscious organizations
not_for: Customer journey analytics without data engineering support (use Segment or mParticle)
Real-time event processing with complex CEP rules (use Flink or Kafka Streams)
Teams needing managed analytics without any data engineering overhead
best_when: A data engineering team wants open-source event collection with full data ownership, no per-event costs, and flexible destination routing as a Segment alternative.
avoid_when: You need a fully managed CDP without operational overhead, or need 300+ pre-built destination connectors out of the box.

auth.notes: Write key for event ingestion (server-side). JavaScript key for client-side tracking (safe to expose). Admin API key for management operations. Keys per data stream. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: completely free; Jitsu Cloud: free up to 200K events/month
pricing.paid_tiers: Jitsu Cloud from $20/month for 1M events; self-hosted: free
pricing.notes: MIT licensed open source — free to self-host. Jitsu Cloud provides managed hosting. US-based. Segment-compatible API for easy migration. Active development. v2 (Jitsu.Next) is significant rewrite — check version compatibility with existing integrations.

performance.rate_limits: Cloud: event volume limits per plan; self-hosted: no limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Simple HTTP event ingestion API — POST /api/v1/event. Segment-compatible track/page/identify endpoints. Management API for streams and destinations. Webhook support. Self-hosted gives full API control.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Event ingestion creates new events — not idempotent. Idempotency key support via messageId field. Stream configuration updates idempotent. Destination management idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: v1 and v2 (Jitsu.Next) have different APIs — verify which version is deployed
Self-hosted requires PostgreSQL and ClickHouse — non-trivial infrastructure
Event validation is permissive — malformed events may reach destinations silently
Destination writes are async — delivery not guaranteed on network errors without retry

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT open source — auditable. GDPR compliant with self-hosted full data control. TLS enforced on cloud. Separate write/admin keys. EU data residency on self-hosted. No formal certifications. Client-side key is safe-to-expose by design.
